output_summary: aligned schemas for datasets: discogs, lastfm, musicbrainz; generated 2 schema correspondence groups.
profiled datasets: discogs, lastfm, musicbrainz.
Reused the blocking configuration from state.
Blocking uses `semantic_similarity` for discogs_lastfm, musicbrainz_lastfm, blocking on name, artist; `sorted_neighbourhood` for discogs_musicbrainz, blocking on name.
Performance-wise, pair completeness ranges from 0.90 to 0.96 and candidate counts range from 47615 to 225831.
The config also records dataset id columns.

matcher mode `rule_based` with config source reused from state; evaluated pairs: id_columns, matching_strategies; selected scores: matching_strategies (threshold=0.6, f1=0.896551724137931, threshold=0.7, f1=0.8732394366197184, threshold=0.75); produced 2 pair strategy entries.
This run created an initial draft of the pipeline adaptation.
The pipeline was built end to end for three CSV datasets named discogs, lastfm, and musicbrainz, including schema matching, blocking, entity matching, correspondence export, and fusion.
LLM-based schema matching was added to rename columns in lastfm and musicbrainz to the discogs schema before downstream processing.
Three pairwise blocking configurations were introduced, using embedding blocking for discogs-lastfm and musicbrainz-lastfm, and sorted neighbourhood blocking for discogs-musicbrainz.
Rule-based entity matching was added for all three dataset pairs with pair-specific comparator sets, weights, and thresholds, and the resulting correspondences were written to CSV files.

A rule-based fusion strategy was added with longest_string for seven scalar attributes and union for three track-level attributes, and the fused output was written to output/data_fusion/fusion_data.csv.
Fusion behavior shifted to a predominantly longest_string policy for core descriptive fields such as name, artist, release-date, release-country, duration, label, and genre.
pipeline execution succeeded on attempt 1; fusion_data.csv was produced.
This run created an initial draft of the evaluation script for the current fused output.
The evaluation used field-specific matching functions registered on a rule-based fusion strategy and evaluated a remapped fused view against the XML gold set by eval_id-to-id alignment.
Name, artist, release-country, label, and genre were evaluated with tokenized_match.
Release-date was evaluated with year_only_match.
Duration was evaluated with numeric_tolerance_match.
Track-level fields such as tracks_track_name, tracks_track_position, and tracks_track_duration were evaluated with set_equality_match.
Any additional detected list-like non-metadata columns were grouped under set_equality_match after normalization.
The run added source-id parsing from _fusion_sources using JSON and ast parsing to recover candidate gold-linked identifiers.

The run added gold-prefix detection and a build_eval_view step that expands fused rows to eval_id candidates, keeps the highest-confidence row per eval_id, and falls back to _id when no expansion is available.
The run added list-like column detection and normalization across fused and gold data while excluding identifier and fusion metadata columns.
evaluation execution succeeded on attempt 1; pipeline_evaluation.json is available.
overall accuracy is 50.76%; evaluation attempt counter is 1.
This run was a refinement of the existing pipeline in evaluation_refinement mode.
The revision adjusted the pipeline after evaluation showed weak overall accuracy and especially poor track-level attribute accuracy.
The pipeline added a normalization stage that standardizes duration, name, artist, release-country, and track list fields before matching and fusion.
Matching changed to use a derived name_norm field for name comparisons in all three matcher configurations.
The discogs-to-musicbrainz matcher replaced a StringComparator on release-date with a DateComparator using a maximum difference of 365 days.
Fusion changed tracks_track_name, tracks_track_position, and tracks_track_duration from union to longest_string.
Before fusion, list-valued track fields are converted into pipe-delimited strings, and duration is converted to a string representation.
The fusion strategy shifted away from set-style aggregation for track attributes toward selecting a single longest string value.
The matching strategy shifted from relying on raw text fields to relying more on normalized representations, especially through name_norm and standardized country and duration values.

df["tracks_track_name"] = df["tracks_track_name"]; fusion_data.csv was produced.
This run was an execution repair of the existing pipeline.

The normalization layer was expanded with a new is_missing helper and a more robust parse_list_like function that now handles tuples, sets, array-like values, literal-evaluated collections, pipe-delimited strings, and comma-delimited strings.
Name normalization was changed to strip artist prefixes from titles by creating name_norm with remove_artist_prefix_from_name instead of using only a simple normalized name function.

Track fields now get explicit matching helper columns named tracks_track_name_match, tracks_track_position_match, and tracks_track_duration_match, and the discogs-to-lastfm matcher was updated to compare tracks_track_name_match instead of the raw tracks_track_name column.
Country normalization was broadened with additional mappings such as england, great britain, gb, usa, and u.s.a. to their canonical forms.
Before fusion, temporary helper columns such as name_norm and track match columns are dropped rather than being left in the fused data preparation step.

pipeline execution failed on attempt 2: error: SyntaxError: Non-UTF-8 code starting with '\x96' in file D:\Studium\Master\Semester 5\Team Project\workspace\data-integration-team-project\agents\output\code\pipeline.py on line 162, but no encoding declared; see https://peps.python.; fusion_data.csv was produced.
This run was an execution repair that reworked the pipeline after a previous SyntaxError caused by non-UTF-8 code.

The fusion stage was changed from generic longest-string selection to attribute-specific fusers for name, artist, label, genre, release-date, release-country, duration, tracks_track_name, tracks_track_position, and tracks_track_duration.

New helper fusers were added to average and stringify duration values, prioritize normalized release countries, prefer more precise release dates, and select fuller track lists for names, positions, and durations.
The matching pipeline was adjusted so the musicbrainz-to-lastfm comparison uses a new normalized duration_str field instead of comparing duration directly as a generic string field.
The run shifted behavior from broadly using longest_string during fusion to field-aware fusion that preserves normalized and structurally richer values.
Fusion now prefers normalized canonical track lists, normalized country aliases, and aggregated numeric duration values rather than whichever raw string is longest.
Name normalization was strengthened by handling underscores, tightening slash normalization, and revising artist-prefix stripping logic before matching.

pipeline execution failed on attempt 3: error: SyntaxError: Non-UTF-8 code starting with '\x96' in file D:\Studium\Master\Semester 5\Team Project\workspace\data-integration-team-project\agents\output\code\pipeline.py on line 142, but no encoding declared; see https://peps.python.; fusion_data.csv was produced.
This run was an execution repair that reworked the pipeline after a previous execution failure caused by a non-UTF-8 syntax error.
The fusion strategy for the name attribute changed from fuse_longest to choose_best_name.
The fusion strategy for genre, label, and artist changed from fuse_longest to choose_most_complete.
Name normalization was strengthened by adding regex-based punctuation and whitespace normalization and by removing articles during matching normalization.

Release date, release country, duration, and track-duration fusion logic were made more selective by preferring structured dates, frequency-based countries, robust duration consensus, and multi-value track-duration candidates.
The pipeline shifted away from length-based fusion toward quality-based selection for textual fields, using choose_best_name for names and choose_most_complete for artist, label, and genre.
Track-name matching shifted to order-insensitive comparison by building tracks_track_name_match with canonical_set_string instead of canonical_list_string.
Artist-prefix stripping in names became regex-based and more constrained, which reduces accidental over-stripping while preserving normalized title matching.
pipeline execution succeeded on attempt 4; fusion_data.csv was produced.
This run was an evaluation refinement rather than an initial draft or a repair.

The dominant strategy remained a field-specific evaluation mapping that used tokenized matching for textual fields, year-only matching for release dates, numeric tolerance matching for duration, and set equality matching for track-list fields.
Name, artist, release-country, label, and genre were grouped under tokenized_match evaluation.
Release-date was evaluated separately with year_only_match.
Duration was evaluated with numeric_tolerance_match as a numeric field.
Tracks_track_name, tracks_track_position, and tracks_track_duration were grouped under set_equality_match, and any additional detected list-like columns also defaulted to set_equality_match.
The run preserved the eval_id alignment workflow built from _fusion_sources, gold-prefix detection, duplicate removal by eval_id, and optional confidence-based sorting before evaluation.
The run preserved list-like column detection and normalization across fused and gold data, excluding identifier and internal confidence fields.
evaluation execution succeeded on attempt 1; pipeline_evaluation.json is available.
overall accuracy is 34.52%; previous logged accuracy was 50.76%; evaluation attempt counter is 2.
This run was a refinement of the existing evaluation pipeline.
The fusion logic for genre and label was changed from `choose_most_complete` to `prefer_nonempty`.
The fusion logic for artist was changed from `choose_most_complete` to `choose_best_artist`.
The helper logic was revised to make duration, release-date, release-country, and track-list fusion more vote-based by using `Counter` and a shared `select_best_track_list` routine.
The canonical serialization for track-position and track-duration lists shifted from pipe-delimited strings to Python list-string formatting via `str(vals)`.
Missing-value handling was broadened to treat `{}`, `na`, and `n/a` as missing values.
The fusion strategy shifted away from favoring the longest or most complete text in several fields and toward taking the first nonempty value or a frequency-backed best value.
Artist fusion became more specialized by preferring a longest sorted artist value rather than using the generic completeness heuristic.
Track-level fusion shifted toward selecting repeated or most common candidate lists through `select_best_track_list` instead of only choosing the longest available list.
pipeline execution succeeded on attempt 1; fusion_data.csv was produced.
This run was an evaluation refinement rather than an initial draft or repair.

The dominant strategy kept the same field-specific evaluators, using tokenized matching for core text fields, numeric tolerance for duration, year-only matching for release-date, and set equality for track-level list fields.
The run also continued to evaluate fused records through an eval_id alignment view derived from _fusion_sources and gold IDs before scoring.
Name, artist, release-country, label, and genre were grouped under tokenized_match evaluation.
Duration was evaluated separately with numeric_tolerance_match, and release-date was evaluated with year_only_match.

Track-level fields including tracks_track_name, tracks_track_position, and tracks_track_duration were grouped under set_equality_match, and any additional detected list-like columns were also assigned set_equality_match.
A new compact_metrics helper was added to recursively round float outputs for printed metrics while leaving the persisted evaluation_results JSON unchanged.
The evaluation strategy name was changed from rule_based_fusion_strategy to rule_based_fusion_strategy_eval without changing the actual per-column evaluation functions.
evaluation execution succeeded on attempt 1; pipeline_evaluation.json is available.
overall accuracy is 51.27%; previous logged accuracy was 34.52%; evaluation attempt counter is 3.
